secondary_fields.field: Quantum field theory in curved spacetime

contribution.title: Hawking radiation
contribution.description: Predicted that black holes emit thermal radiation due to quantum effects near the event horizon, with temperature T = hbar c^3 / (8 pi G M k_B).
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Defines the speculative_extension boundary for this project. Hawking radiation is well-established theoretically but unobserved.

contribution.title: Four laws of black hole mechanics
contribution.description: Co-developed (with Bardeen and Carter) the formal analogy between black hole mechanics and the laws of thermodynamics.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Anchors the connection between geometry (surface gravity, horizon area) and thermodynamic-style quantities (temperature, entropy).

contribution.title: Penrose-Hawking singularity theorems
contribution.description: Generalized Penrose's theorem to broader contexts including cosmological singularities.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Same as Penrose's contribution: bounds the truth-tier of interior claims.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Quantum field theory in fixed curved backgrounds; gain insight by combining geometry with QFT modes.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What do the field modes look like near this horizon?
What does an observer at infinity see, vs a freely falling observer?
Is there a thermodynamic analog of this geometric quantity?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Treating QFT-in-curved-spacetime results as final theory of quantum gravity (they are not).

reference.citation: Hawking, S. W. (1974). Black hole explosions? Nature 248, 30.
Bardeen, J. M., Carter, B., Hawking, S. W. (1973). The four laws of black hole mechanics. Comm. Math. Phys. 31, 161.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Reasoning about Hawking radiation, black-hole thermodynamics, the information paradox, or the boundary between classical GR and quantum gravity.
how_to_apply.cautions: Hawking radiation has not been observed. Any code path or visualization implying detection should be labeled speculative_extension.
